lede: The AirTrain connects all passenger terminals at JFK Airport. If your connecting flight departs from a different terminal, use the AirTrain to get to your next terminal. The AirTrain is free for trips between terminals. Check whether your connecting flight departs from the same terminal you are arriving at. Many airlines codeshare connecting flights. Make sure you check the terminal of the operating carrier. For example, if you booked through Finnair but your flight is operated by American Airlines, you’ll need to go to the American Airlines terminal. If you need to change terminals, follow airport signs to the AirTrain and to your next terminal. Once you get to your departure terminal, you will need to go through TSA security screening again. If you are arriving on an international flight: Collect your checked bags at baggage claim, even if they are tagged to your final destination. Deposit your bags at the baggage recheck facility before heading to your next flight.

paragraphs: JFK has multiple terminals, so you may arrive at one terminal and depart from another. Please budget additional travel time as you will need to go through the TSA screening process again. All terminals are connected by AirTrain, which you can take for free between terminals. Be sure to check your airline app or in-terminal monitors for your next gate.
Connecting at a different local airport? Review our traveling between airports guide.

links.text: Review our traveling between airports guide

paragraphs: Plan your journey using our interactive map opens in a new tab. When you arrive at JFK, we encourage you to visit our departures guide, to view current security wait times and walk times to gates.

blocks.h2: Get to your connecting flight on AirTrain
paragraphs: The AirTrain connects all passenger terminals at JFK Airport. If your connecting flight departs from a different terminal, use the AirTrain to get to your next terminal. The AirTrain is free for trips between terminals.
If you are arriving on an international flight:

links.text: Make sure you check the terminal

paragraphs: Delta offers a free shuttle bus between concourses within Terminal 4 to minimize walking distances. You will not need to go through TSA security again.
Note that the shuttle is not necessarily a faster option than walking.
Terminal 4 shuttle routes (PDF) opens in a new tab

links.text: Terminal 4 shuttle routes (PDF) opens in a new tab

blocks.h2: Make the most of your layover at JFK
h3s: Grab a snack, pick up a gift, or take a break
Find carts, cash, and baggage help
Airport hotels make long layovers easier
paragraphs: Before you leave JFK, stop by for a quick bite, grab a last-minute gift, or relax at one of our lounges or shops.
After you arrive at JFK, you can find luggage carts, ATMs, porter services, and help with baggage issues. For lost or delayed bags, contact your airline first, then visit a baggage office if needed.
If you have a long connection time at JFK, book an airport hotel to get some rest and freshen up between flights.
